questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول: المضارع البسيط يتحول الى الماضي البسيط
questions.question: Choose the correct reported speech for this direct statement.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I have a brother and a sister.' Reported: He said (that) he _____ a brother and a sister.
questions.explanation: In reported speech, the simple present (have) shifts back to simple past (had). The reporting verb 'said' signals this backshift.

questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول: المضارع البسيط المنفي يتحول الى الماضي البسيط المنفي
questions.question: Select the correct reported form of this negative sentence.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I don't like mangoes.' Reported: She said (that) she _____ mangoes.
questions.explanation: The simple present negative (don't like) shifts to simple past negative (didn't like) in reported speech.

questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول: المضارع المستمر يتحول الى الماضي المستمر
questions.question: Choose the correct reported form.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I'm talking to Sara.' Reported: She said she _____ to Sara.
questions.explanation: The present progressive (am/is/are + -ing) shifts to past progressive (was/were + -ing) in reported speech.

questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول: الماضي البسيط يتحول الى الماضي التام
questions.question: Select the correct reported form of this past tense sentence.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I learned English in Canada.' Reported: He said he _____ English in Canada.
questions.explanation: The simple past (learned) shifts to past perfect (had learned) when reporting what someone said.

questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول: الحاضر التام يتحول الى الماضي التام
questions.question: Choose the correct reported form of this present perfect sentence.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I haven't seen the film yet.' Reported: She said she _____ the film yet.
questions.explanation: The present perfect (haven't seen) shifts to past perfect (hadn't seen) in reported speech.

questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول: will يتحول الى would
questions.question: Select the correct reported form for a sentence with 'will.'
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I'll see you later.' Reported: She said she _____ see them later.
questions.explanation: 'Will' changes to 'would' in reported speech. This is a standard modal backshift.

questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول: can't يتحول الى couldn't
questions.question: Choose the correct reported form for a sentence with 'can't.'
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I can't come to the meeting.' Reported: He said he _____ come to the meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Can't' (cannot) shifts to 'couldn't' in reported speech. Modal verbs generally shift one step back into the past.

questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول: must/have to يتحول الى had to
questions.question: Select the correct reported form for 'must' expressing obligation.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I have to go to the doctor.' Reported: She said she _____ go to the doctor.
questions.explanation: Both 'must' and 'have to' (when expressing necessity) shift to 'had to' in reported speech.

questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول: may يتحول الى might
questions.question: Choose the reported form for a sentence using 'may.'
questions.prompt: Direct: 'We may be late.' Reported: I said that we _____ be late.
questions.explanation: 'May' (expressing possibility) shifts to 'might' in reported speech.

questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول: حذف that بعد said
questions.question: Identify which sentence is correct — 'that' after 'said' is optional.
questions.prompt: Which sentence is grammatically correct?
options: He said that he was tired.
Both A and B are correct.
Neither A nor B is correct.
questions.answer: Both A and B are correct.
questions.explanation: The word 'that' is optional after 'said' in reported speech. Both 'He said that he was tired' and 'He said he was tired' are correct.

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة المنقولة: كلمة الاستفهام تبقى في الجملة المنقولة
questions.question: Choose the correct reported question form.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'How old are you?' Reported: He asked _____ I was.
questions.explanation: In reported questions with a question word (how, where, when, etc.), the question word stays — but the subject and verb do NOT invert. No 'if' is needed.

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة المنقولة: ترتيب الكلمات بدون انقلاب (فعل بعد الفاعل)
questions.question: Select the correctly ordered reported question.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'Where were you last night?' Reported: She wanted to know where _____ the night before.
questions.explanation: In reported questions, there is no subject-verb inversion — the subject comes before the verb. 'Last night' also shifts to 'the night before.' Past simple 'were' shifts to past perfect 'had been.'

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة المنقولة بدون كلمة استفهام: نستخدم if
questions.question: Choose the correct reported form when there is no question word.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'Are you a student?' Reported: He asked _____ Tom was a student.
questions.explanation: When there is no question word (who, what, where, etc.), use 'if' or 'whether' to introduce a reported yes/no question.

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة المنقولة بدون كلمة استفهام: Did يتحول الى had
questions.question: Select the correct reported form of this yes/no question.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'Did you enjoy the dinner party?' Reported: She asked if they _____ the dinner party.
questions.explanation: Simple past (enjoyed) shifts to past perfect (had enjoyed) in reported questions. 'If' introduces the question since there is no question word.

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة المنقولة: ترتيب الكلمات الصحيح
questions.question: Choose the correctly structured reported question.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'When does the library close?' Reported: He asked _____ .
questions.explanation: In indirect/reported questions, the word order is statement-style (subject + verb), not question-style. No auxiliary verb inversion.

questions.arabicHint: تغيير الضمائر في الكلام المنقول: I يتحول الى he/she
questions.question: Select the correct pronoun in this reported statement.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I did my homework already.' Reported: Todd said that _____ had done _____ homework already.
questions.explanation: In reported speech, the pronoun 'I' changes to match the subject of the reporting clause. Since Todd is male, 'I' becomes 'he' and 'my' becomes 'his.'

questions.arabicHint: تغيير ظروف الزمان في الكلام المنقول: now يتحول الى at that time/then
questions.question: Choose the correct time expression change in reported speech.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'The plane is arriving now.' Reported: Mary said that the plane was arriving _____.
questions.explanation: 'Now' changes to 'then' or 'at that time' in reported speech because the speaker is no longer at the original moment of speaking.

questions.arabicHint: تغيير ظروف الزمان: today يتحول الى that day
questions.question: Select the correct time word change from direct to reported speech.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I'm finishing the project today.' Reported: She said she was finishing the project _____.
questions.explanation: 'Today' changes to 'that day' in reported speech. Time expressions shift to maintain logical accuracy when reporting later.

questions.arabicHint: تغيير ظروف الزمان: tomorrow يتحول الى the next day/the day after
questions.question: Choose the correct time expression for reported speech.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I'll call you tomorrow.' Reported: He said he would call me _____.
questions.explanation: 'Tomorrow' changes to 'the next day' or 'the day after' in reported speech.

questions.arabicHint: تغيير ظروف الزمان: yesterday يتحول الى the day before
questions.question: Select the correct time expression change.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I met her yesterday.' Reported: He said he had met her _____.
questions.explanation: 'Yesterday' changes to 'the day before' in reported speech, since the time reference point has shifted.

questions.arabicHint: افعال الابلاغ: tell يختلف عن say (يتبعه مفعول به غير مباشر)
questions.question: Identify which sentence correctly uses 'tell.'
questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct?
options: He told that he was ready.
He told me that he was ready.
He said me that he was ready.
He told to me that he was ready.
questions.answer: He told me that he was ready.
questions.explanation: 'Tell' always requires an indirect object (me, him, her, us, them). You say 'tell someone something.' Never use 'told that' without an object.

questions.arabicHint: فعل الابلاغ: agree (للموافقة)
questions.question: Choose the correct reporting verb for this sentence.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'Yes, it's a great idea.' Reported: My friend _____ it was a great idea.
questions.explanation: 'Agree' is a reporting verb used when someone confirms or accepts an idea. It conveys the meaning more precisely than just 'said.'

questions.arabicHint: فعل الابلاغ: assure (للتاكيد مع مفعول به)
questions.question: Select the correct reporting verb — it requires a direct object and means 'to promise confidently.'
questions.prompt: Tom _____ the teacher that he had done his homework.
questions.explanation: 'Assure' means to tell someone confidently to remove doubt. Like 'tell,' it takes a direct object. 'He assured the teacher' — not 'he assured that.'

questions.arabicHint: said vs told: الفرق في الاستخدام
questions.question: Choose the sentence that is grammatically correct.
questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses a reporting verb?
options: She said me that she was leaving.
She told that she was leaving.
She told us that she was leaving.
She said to us that she was leaving us.
questions.answer: She told us that she was leaving.
questions.explanation: 'Tell' requires a direct object (us, him, me). 'Said' does not take a direct object — you say something but tell someone something. Only option C is correct.

questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول المتكامل: اختر الجملة الصحيحة بالكامل
questions.question: Select the fully correct reported speech sentence.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I'll visit you tomorrow.' Reported: ___________
options: She said she will visit me tomorrow.
She said she would visit me the next day.
She said she would visit me tomorrow.
She told she would visit me the next day.
questions.answer: She said she would visit me the next day.
questions.explanation: 'Will' shifts to 'would,' 'tomorrow' shifts to 'the next day,' and 'you' shifts to 'me.' 'Said' does not need an object. Option B is the only fully correct form.

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة السلبية: للتعبير عن الدهشة او التحقق
questions.question: Choose the correct negative question for this context.
questions.prompt: You're surprised your friend doesn't know the news. You ask: '_____?'
questions.explanation: Negative questions are used to express surprise or check information. 'Haven't you seen the news?' implies the speaker expected the listener to have seen it.

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة السلبية: Aren't / Isn't / Don't / Didn't
questions.question: Select the correct negative question expressing surprise.
questions.prompt: You see your friend standing still while everyone is helping. You say: '_____ going to help?'
questions.explanation: 'Aren't you going to help?' is a negative question that expresses surprise or mild criticism. It implies the speaker expected the person to help.

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة السلبية: استخدامها للتعبير عن عدم التصديق
questions.question: Choose the negative question that best expresses disbelief.
questions.prompt: You can't believe nobody told your friend about the flood. You ask: '_____ about the damage?'
questions.explanation: 'Didn't you ask them about the damage?' is a negative question implying the speaker expected the listener to have asked. It expresses surprise or disbelief.

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة السلبية: Isn't there للتعبير عن الاستنكار
questions.question: Select the correct negative question that implies there should be a solution.
questions.prompt: '_____ something we can do about the pollution?' the reporter asked.
questions.explanation: 'Isn't there something we can do?' is a negative question expressing frustration and implying the speaker believes something should be done.

questions.arabicHint: ظرف المكان النسبي where: يصف مكانا
questions.question: Choose the correct relative adverb to complete the sentence.
questions.prompt: That's the school _____ I used to study as a child.
questions.explanation: Use 'where' as a relative adverb to describe a place. 'That's the school where I studied' — 'where' replaces 'at which' when referring to a location.

questions.arabicHint: where كظرف نسبي يرتبط بمكان
questions.question: Select the correct relative adverb.
questions.prompt: Riyadh is the city _____ I was born and raised.
questions.explanation: 'Where' is used as a relative adverb when referring to a place. Here it describes the city as the place of the speaker's birth and upbringing.

questions.arabicHint: where في الجمل المعبرة عن المكان المجازي
questions.question: Choose the best completion using 'where' as a relative adverb.
questions.prompt: Home is _____ the heart is — that's what the old saying tells us.
questions.explanation: 'Home is where the heart is' is a famous expression using 'where' to connect 'home' with the idea of belonging. 'Where' here acts as a relative adverb of place.

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة غير المباشرة: ترتيب الكلمات بدون انقلاب
questions.question: Choose the correct indirect question form.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'Where is the nearest hospital?' Indirect: Do you know _____ ?
questions.explanation: In indirect questions, use statement word order (subject + verb), not question order. No auxiliary inversion: 'where the nearest hospital is' — not 'where is the nearest hospital.'

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة غير المباشرة: Do you know + statement order
questions.question: Select the correctly formed indirect question.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'When does the store open?' Indirect: Do you know _____ ?
questions.explanation: Indirect questions use statement word order. The auxiliary 'does' disappears and the main verb takes -s: 'when the store opens.'

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة غير المباشرة: Could you tell me + statement order
questions.question: Choose the correct indirect question using 'Could you tell me.'
questions.prompt: Direct: 'Where can I get good pizza?' Indirect: Could you tell me _____ ?
options: where can I get good pizza
where I can get good pizza
if I can get good pizza where
where good pizza I can get
questions.answer: where I can get good pizza
questions.explanation: Indirect questions follow statement word order: subject (I) before verb (can get). The question word 'where' stays, but inversion is removed.

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة غير المباشرة: Do you know what + ترتيب الجملة
questions.question: Select the correct indirect question.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'What is the name of the street?' Indirect: Do you know _____ ?
options: what is the name of the street
what the name of the street is
that the name of the street is
if the name of the street is what
questions.answer: what the name of the street is
questions.explanation: Statement order applies: 'what the name of the street is' — the verb 'is' comes after the subject 'the name of the street,' not before it.

questions.arabicHint: التعبير عن الموافقة على جملة مثبتة: So + فعل مساعد + فاعل
questions.question: Choose the correct agreement expression for an affirmative statement.
questions.prompt: A: 'I ate at the new restaurant last night.' B: '_____'
questions.explanation: Use 'So + auxiliary + subject' to agree with an affirmative statement. 'So did I' means 'I also ate there.' The auxiliary matches the tense of the original statement.

questions.arabicHint: التعبير عن الموافقة على جملة مثبتة: I did too / I will too
questions.question: Select the alternative agreement form for affirmative statements.
questions.prompt: A: 'I will visit my family this weekend.' B: '_____'
options: Both A and B are correct.
questions.answer: Both A and B are correct.
questions.explanation: Both 'So will I' and 'I will too' are correct ways to agree with an affirmative statement. They are interchangeable and equally acceptable.

questions.arabicHint: التعبير عن الموافقة على جملة منفية: Neither + فعل مساعد + فاعل
questions.question: Choose the correct agreement expression for a negative statement.
questions.prompt: A: 'I didn't like the pizza.' B: '_____'
questions.explanation: Use 'Neither + auxiliary + subject' to agree with a negative statement. 'Neither did I' means 'I also didn't like it.'

questions.arabicHint: التعبير عن الموافقة على جملة منفية: I don't either / I won't either
questions.question: Select the alternative agreement form for negative statements.
questions.prompt: A: 'I won't go to school tomorrow.' B: '_____'
options: Both A and B are correct.
questions.answer: Both A and B are correct.
questions.explanation: Both 'Neither will I' and 'I won't either' correctly express agreement with a negative statement. They carry exactly the same meaning.

questions.arabicHint: التعبير عن الاختلاف مع جملة مثبتة
questions.question: Choose the correct disagreement response to an affirmative statement.
questions.prompt: A: 'I've been to Muscat.' B: '_____' (the speaker has NOT been to Muscat)
questions.explanation: To disagree with an affirmative statement, use the opposite auxiliary in the negative. If A has been, but B hasn't — B says 'I haven't.' This shows polite disagreement.

questions.arabicHint: التعبير عن الاختلاف مع جملة منفية
questions.question: Select the correct disagreement response to a negative statement.
questions.prompt: A: 'I am not very tall.' B: '_____' (the speaker IS quite tall)
questions.explanation: To disagree with a negative statement, use the opposite auxiliary in the affirmative. If A is not tall but B is — B says 'I am.' Short affirmative answers show polite disagreement.

questions.arabicHint: So / Neither: اختيار الصحيح حسب نوع الجملة
questions.question: Choose the correct agreement expression for each blank.
questions.prompt: A: 'I love science.' B: '_____.' / A: 'I don't know the answer.' B: '_____.'
options: So do I / Neither do I
Neither do I / So do I
So do I / So do I
Neither do I / Neither do I
questions.answer: So do I / Neither do I
questions.explanation: 'So do I' agrees with an affirmative (I love science). 'Neither do I' agrees with a negative (I don't know). The expressions must match the polarity of the original statement.

questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول الكامل: تطبيق مجموعة من القواعد
questions.question: Select the fully correct reported speech sentence.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I can't find my keys.' Reported: She said _____ .
questions.explanation: 'Can't' shifts to 'couldn't.' 'My' changes to 'her' (pronoun shift). The meaning stays the same. All changes must be applied together.

questions.arabicHint: الاسئلة المنقولة الكاملة: تطبيق جميع القواعد
questions.question: Choose the fully correct reported question.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'Did you finish the report yesterday?' Reported: She asked him _____ .
options: if he finished the report yesterday
if he had finished the report the day before
if did he finish the report the day before
that he had finished the report the day before
questions.answer: if he had finished the report the day before
questions.explanation: Yes/no questions use 'if.' Past simple (finished) shifts to past perfect (had finished). 'Yesterday' shifts to 'the day before.' Pronoun 'you' shifts to 'he.' Statement word order is used.

questions.arabicHint: التغيير من المخاطب الى الغائب في الكلام المنقول
questions.question: Select the correct pronoun changes in reported speech.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'We may be late for the meeting.' Reported: They said that _____ might be late for the meeting.
questions.explanation: 'We' changes to 'they' in reported speech when someone reports what a group of other people said. The pronoun shifts to match the third person perspective.

questions.arabicHint: السؤال غير المباشر: هل تعرف اين / متى / كيف
questions.question: Choose the correct indirect question.
questions.prompt: You want to know if there's a pharmacy nearby. You ask a stranger: '_____?'
options: Do you know where is there a pharmacy?
Do you know where there is a pharmacy?
Do you know where a pharmacy there is?
Could you know where is a pharmacy?
questions.answer: Do you know where there is a pharmacy?
questions.explanation: Indirect questions use statement word order after the question word. 'Where there is a pharmacy' (not 'where is there') is the correct structure.

questions.arabicHint: الموافقة والاختلاف: تطبيق متقدم
questions.question: Choose the correct response that expresses agreement with Speaker A.
questions.prompt: A: 'I've never tried sushi.' B: '_____' (B also hasn't tried sushi)
questions.explanation: 'Neither have I' agrees with a negative statement using present perfect. 'I haven't too' is incorrect — use 'either' not 'too' with negatives. 'I haven't either' would also be correct.

questions.arabicHint: الكلام المنقول: جملة كاملة متعددة التحولات
questions.question: Select the sentence where ALL reported speech changes are correctly applied.
questions.prompt: Direct: 'I'll see you tomorrow.' (said by Layla to her friend) Reported: Layla said _____ .
options: she will see her friend tomorrow
she would see her friend the next day
she would see you the next day
she sees her friend the next day
questions.answer: she would see her friend the next day
questions.explanation: 'Will' shifts to 'would.' 'You' shifts to 'her friend' (perspective change). 'Tomorrow' shifts to 'the next day.' All three changes must happen simultaneously.

questions.arabicHint: السؤال غير المباشر المتكامل: Could you tell me + statement order
questions.question: Choose the only fully correct indirect question.
questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct?
options: Could you tell me where can I find the manager?
Could you tell me where I can find the manager?
Could you tell me where I could found the manager?
Could you tell me if where I can find the manager?
questions.answer: Could you tell me where I can find the manager?
questions.explanation: Indirect questions use statement word order after the question word: subject (I) + verb (can find). No inversion of 'can' and 'I.' No extra 'if' when a question word is already present.

questions.arabicHint: شخص يتصل هاتفيا للترويج لمنتجات
questions.prompt: Many people find _____ annoying because they call at inconvenient times to sell products or services.
questions.explanation: Telemarketers are people who call potential customers by phone to sell products or services. Many people consider them a nuisance.

questions.arabicHint: ازعاج / مصدر انزعاج
questions.prompt: Constant noise from the street outside was a real _____ for the students trying to study.
questions.explanation: A nuisance is something or someone that causes annoyance or inconvenience. Telemarketers are often described as a nuisance.

questions.arabicHint: يقنع / يحاول ان يجعل شخصا يفعل شيئا
questions.prompt: The salesperson tried to _____ the customer to buy the more expensive model by highlighting its features.
questions.explanation: To persuade means to cause someone to do something through reasoning or argument. Telemarketers often try to persuade people to buy their products.

questions.arabicHint: يصر / لا يتراجع عن موقفه
questions.prompt: Even after being told no three times, the salesman _____ and kept repeating his offer.
questions.explanation: To insist means to continue demanding something firmly, even when faced with refusal. A pushy salesperson insists even when the customer says no.

questions.arabicHint: متسرع / عدواني في اسلوب البيع
questions.prompt: The agent was very _____ — he wouldn't take no for an answer and kept interrupting.
questions.explanation: Pushy describes someone who is aggressively trying to get what they want. A pushy salesperson makes customers feel pressured and uncomfortable.

questions.arabicHint: يغلق الخط / ينهي المكالمة
questions.prompt: Frustrated by the caller, she finally _____ without saying goodbye.
questions.explanation: To hang up means to end a phone call. It can also suggest rudely ending a call mid-conversation when you have run out of patience.

questions.arabicHint: يتصل بـ (عبر الهاتف)
questions.prompt: He _____ his old friend after not speaking to him for over two years.
questions.explanation: 'Call up' means to telephone someone. It is an informal phrasal verb commonly used in everyday conversation.

questions.arabicHint: خداع مدبر / حيلة مزيفة
questions.prompt: The email claiming he had won a lottery was a complete _____ — he had never bought a ticket.
questions.explanation: A hoax is a trick or deception designed to make someone believe something false. 'You won a prize' scams are a classic example of a hoax.

questions.arabicHint: مسابقة / منافسة يفوز فيها شخص بجائزة
questions.prompt: She entered an online _____ and was excited when she received a notification saying she had come first.
questions.explanation: A contest is a competition where people try to win a prize. If someone claims you won without entering a contest, it is likely a hoax.

questions.arabicHint: يعتذر / يقول اسف
questions.prompt: He felt terrible about being late, so he _____ sincerely and promised it would not happen again.
questions.explanation: To apologize means to say sorry and acknowledge a mistake. Apologizing is an important part of maintaining good relationships.

questions.arabicHint: جهاز الرد على المكالمات
questions.prompt: No one was home, so the caller left a message on the family's _____.
questions.explanation: An answering machine is a device that records telephone messages when no one is available to answer. Families use them to never miss a call.

questions.arabicHint: رسالة صوتية تركها شخص على الهاتف
questions.prompt: She forgot her phone and came home to find three _____ from her manager.
questions.explanation: A message is information left for someone when they are unavailable. Phone messages are a common way to communicate when a call is not answered.

questions.arabicHint: وكالة السفر: شركة تنظم رحلات سياحية
questions.prompt: He booked his flight and hotel through a _____ that specialised in last-minute holiday deals.
questions.explanation: A travel agency is a business that arranges holidays, flights, and accommodation for customers. Scammers sometimes pretend to be from travel agencies.

questions.arabicHint: منطقة جبلية ذات تضاريس وعرة
questions.prompt: The hiking trail passes through a beautiful _____ region with steep cliffs and cool air.
questions.explanation: Mountainous means covered with mountains or having a landscape of steep hills. Cold, mountainous destinations require warm clothing.

questions.arabicHint: حساب مصرفي: سجل في البنك
questions.prompt: She went to the bank to _____ a new savings _____ and was given a welcome pack.
questions.explanation: 'Open an account' means to start a new banking arrangement. Telemarketers often try to persuade people to open accounts with their banks.

questions.arabicHint: في مكان اخر / عند مزود مختلف
questions.prompt: He politely told the salesman he already had similar services _____ and was not interested.
questions.explanation: Elsewhere means somewhere other than the place being mentioned. Saying 'I have an account elsewhere' means you already use a different provider.

questions.arabicHint: يفقد صبره / لا يستطيع الانتظار اكثر
questions.prompt: After waiting an hour with no response, he finally _____ his patience and demanded to speak to a manager.
questions.explanation: 'Lose one's patience' means to stop being patient and become frustrated. When a caller is too pushy, people often lose patience and simply hang up.

questions.arabicHint: في النهاية / كنتيجة اخيرة
questions.prompt: After hours of negotiation, _____, both sides agreed to the terms of the deal.
questions.explanation: 'In the end' is used to say what finally happened after a long process or series of events. It is similar in meaning to 'finally' or 'eventually.'

questions.arabicHint: او ما شابه ذلك: للاشارة الى اشياء مشابهة
questions.prompt: Have you ever entered a competition, a raffle, _____?
questions.explanation: 'Or anything like that' is an informal phrase meaning 'or other similar things.' It avoids giving a complete list and keeps conversation natural and relaxed.

questions.arabicHint: تسويق عبر الهاتف
questions.prompt: She strongly believed that _____ should be banned, as it invades people's privacy at home.
questions.explanation: Telemarketing is the practice of marketing products or services directly to potential customers by telephone. Many countries have laws restricting it.

questions.arabicHint: الاحترار العالمي: ارتفاع حرارة الارض
questions.prompt: Scientists warn that _____ is causing ice caps to melt and sea levels to rise around the world.
questions.explanation: Global warming is the long-term rise in Earth's average temperature due to greenhouse gas emissions. It is one of the most serious environmental issues today.

questions.arabicHint: تآكل التربة: اختفاء الطبقة الخارجية من الارض
questions.prompt: Heavy rain and cutting down trees led to severe _____ of the hillside soil.
questions.explanation: Erosion is the gradual wearing away of soil or rock by wind, water, or other natural forces. Deforestation makes erosion much worse.

questions.arabicHint: تلوث الهواء: وجود مواد ضارة في الجو
questions.prompt: Factory smoke and vehicle exhaust are major causes of _____ in modern cities.
questions.explanation: Air pollution is the presence of harmful substances in the air, caused by burning fuel, factories, and vehicles. It is a major environmental and health threat.

questions.arabicHint: الصيد المفرط: اصطياد الاسماك باكثر مما ينبغي
questions.prompt: Marine biologists warned that _____ was pushing several species of fish towards extinction.
questions.explanation: Overfishing means catching fish at a rate faster than they can reproduce. It threatens ocean biodiversity and the livelihoods of fishing communities.

questions.arabicHint: ازالة الغابات: قطع الاشجار على نطاق واسع
questions.prompt: Large areas of rainforest disappear every year due to _____ for farming and logging.
questions.explanation: Deforestation is the large-scale removal of forests. It destroys habitats, contributes to climate change, and accelerates soil erosion.

questions.arabicHint: فيضان: غمر الارض بالمياه
questions.prompt: The river burst its banks after three days of heavy rain, causing a severe _____ in the valley below.
questions.explanation: A flood occurs when a large amount of water covers an area that is usually dry. Heavy rain, burst riverbanks, or storms can cause devastating flooding.

questions.arabicHint: زلزال: اهتزاز الارض فجاة
questions.prompt: The buildings collapsed after a powerful _____ struck the coastal city early in the morning.
questions.explanation: An earthquake is a sudden and violent shaking of the ground caused by movement of tectonic plates. It is one of the most destructive natural disasters.

questions.arabicHint: تسونامي: موجة بحرية ضخمة ناتجة عن زلازل
questions.prompt: The underwater earthquake triggered a powerful _____ that swept several kilometres inland.
questions.explanation: A tsunami is a series of giant ocean waves usually caused by an undersea earthquake or volcanic eruption. It can devastate coastal areas within minutes.

questions.arabicHint: ثوران بركاني: انفجار بركان
questions.prompt: Lava and ash covered the surrounding villages after the sudden _____.
questions.explanation: A volcanic eruption occurs when magma, ash, and gases burst from a volcano. It is one of the most dramatic and destructive natural events.

questions.arabicHint: حريق الغابات: النيران التي تمتد في الاشجار
questions.prompt: Dry conditions and strong winds caused a _____ that spread across hundreds of acres.
questions.explanation: A forest fire is an uncontrolled fire that spreads through a wooded area. Climate change and deforestation increase the risk of forest fires worldwide.

questions.arabicHint: ضرر / دمار: الاذى الناتج عن كارثة
questions.prompt: The hurricane caused significant _____ to roads, homes, and power lines across the region.
questions.explanation: Damage refers to physical harm that reduces the value or usefulness of something. Natural disasters typically cause massive damage to infrastructure.

questions.arabicHint: يهدد / يشكل خطرا على شيء او شخص
questions.prompt: Rising sea levels _____ low-lying islands, which may disappear entirely within decades.
questions.explanation: To threaten means to put someone or something at risk of harm. Environmental problems like flooding and erosion threaten entire communities worldwide.

questions.arabicHint: ينقذ / يخرج شخصا من خطر
questions.prompt: Emergency crews worked through the night to _____ survivors trapped under the rubble.
questions.explanation: To rescue means to save someone from a dangerous or harmful situation. Rescue teams respond immediately after earthquakes, floods, and other disasters.

questions.arabicHint: يحمي / يصون شيئا من الاذى
questions.prompt: New laws were introduced to _____ endangered species from being hunted.
questions.explanation: To protect means to keep something or someone safe from harm or damage. Environmental laws protect wildlife, forests, and clean water sources.

questions.arabicHint: بحث علمي: دراسة منهجية لفهم شيء ما
questions.prompt: The fishing industry claimed it was catching whales for _____ purposes, not for food.
questions.explanation: Research means careful study and investigation to discover new facts or test ideas. Some industries claim research as a justification for controversial activities.

questions.arabicHint: مثالي / يستحق التقليد
questions.prompt: The teacher was described as _____ — always fair, well-prepared, and dedicated to every student.
questions.explanation: Exemplary means serving as a perfect example worthy of imitation. A leader who is exemplary inspires others through their consistent and admirable actions.

questions.arabicHint: مسعى / هدف يسعى اليه شخص بجهد كبير
questions.prompt: Space exploration is a remarkable scientific _____ that has pushed the limits of human knowledge.
questions.explanation: An endeavor is a serious attempt or effort to do something important. It is often used for ambitious projects requiring significant commitment and resources.

questions.arabicHint: يتحكم في / يسيطر على
questions.prompt: A truly wise person _____ their emotions rather than letting emotions control their decisions.
questions.explanation: To govern means to control or manage. Benjamin Franklin suggested that true power lies in governing one's own passions.

questions.arabicHint: الرغبات / العواطف القوية
questions.prompt: Benjamin Franklin suggested that a truly powerful person is one who controls their _____.
questions.explanation: Passions are strong emotions or desires. Franklin's quote implies that self-control over one's own desires is a truer form of power than wealth or status.

questions.arabicHint: راضٍ بما لديه / مكتفٍ
questions.prompt: According to Franklin's wisdom, the truly rich person is one who is _____ with what they have.
questions.explanation: Content means satisfied and at ease with one's situation. Franklin's quote equates being content with being wealthy, suggesting richness is a state of mind.

questions.arabicHint: ناشط / شخص يكافح من اجل قضية
questions.prompt: Helen Keller was a famous _____ who campaigned for the rights of people with disabilities.
questions.explanation: An activist is a person who campaigns actively for social or political change. Helen Keller used her story and voice to inspire others and fight for important causes.

questions.arabicHint: رؤية / قدرة على التصور المستقبلي او الهادف
questions.prompt: Helen Keller said it is terrible to see and have no _____ — meaning eyes without purpose are wasted.
questions.explanation: Vision here means the ability to think about the future with imagination and wisdom. Keller's quote suggests that physical sight without insight or purpose is meaningless.

questions.arabicHint: دبلوماسي: شخص يمثل بلده في الخارج
questions.prompt: Benjamin Franklin served as a _____ in France, representing American interests during a critical period of history.
questions.explanation: A diplomat is an official who represents their country in foreign relations. Franklin was one of America's most celebrated diplomats, known for his charm and intelligence.

questions.arabicHint: مؤسس شركة: الشخص الذي انشاها لاول مرة
questions.prompt: Bill Gates, the _____ of Microsoft, famously misjudged how much computer memory people would ever need.
questions.explanation: A founder is the person who establishes an organisation, company, or movement for the first time. Bill Gates co-founded Microsoft in 1975.

questions.arabicHint: التعليم الرسمي: الدراسة في المدارس والجامعات
questions.prompt: Mark Twain famously warned against letting _____ get in the way of real learning.
questions.explanation: Formal education refers to structured learning in schools and universities. Twain's quote suggests real wisdom also comes from life experience, not just academic instruction.

questions.arabicHint: يتكون من / مكون من اجزاء متعددة
questions.prompt: Robert Frost's quote suggests that the world is _____ two types of people: those who talk too much and those who say nothing.
questions.explanation: 'Composed of' means made up of or consisting of. Frost's quote humorously divides humanity into two contrasting types of communicators.

questions.arabicHint: الصيدلية: محل بيع الادوية
questions.prompt: She asked a passerby if they knew where the nearest _____ was, as she needed to buy medicine urgently.
questions.explanation: A pharmacy is a shop where medicinal drugs are sold and dispensed. Asking for directions to the nearest pharmacy is a classic indirect question context.

questions.arabicHint: مكتب البريد: مكان ارسال الطرود والرسائل
questions.prompt: He needed to send a package abroad, so he asked if anyone knew where the main _____ was.
questions.explanation: A post office is a place where you can send letters and parcels, buy stamps, and access postal services. It is frequently used in indirect question exercises about directions.

questions.arabicHint: محطة وقود: مكان تعبئة الوقود
questions.prompt: She asked the hotel receptionist if they knew when the nearest _____ closed.
questions.explanation: A gas station (or petrol station) is a facility where vehicles are refuelled. Knowing when one closes is important for travellers on long road trips.

questions.arabicHint: حكيم / يتبع تصرفات ذكية وعقلانية
questions.prompt: Benjamin Franklin asked: 'Who is _____? He that learns from everyone.' This suggests wisdom comes from openness.
questions.explanation: Wise means having experience, knowledge, and good judgement. Franklin's quote defines wisdom as the ability to learn from all people around you.
